conversations.title: System of Equations — Frontend Review & Backend Alignment
conversations.summary: Reviewed existing System of Equations lesson (5 levels). Fixed 3 frontend issues and 2 backend misconfigurations.
changes: Fixed hardcoded colors in EqChip (#3B82F6/#EF4444) and HintBox (#f6ad55) to use theme tokens — dark mode compliance
Restructured SystemBox layout: brace now on left side with equations stacked vertically (standard math notation)
Fixed Level 4 substitution explanation to properly format negative coefficients (was showing '+ -10y', now shows '− 10y')
Updated backend config from 1 level to 5 levels with component names
Updated getLessonDataInitial.js: replaced single BasicProblemsWordsOnly with all 5 SystemOfEquations components and level-specific hints

conversations.title: System of Equations — Migrate to Backend Data Generation
conversations.summary: Migrated System of Equations from pure frontend generation to backend data generation, following the PythagoreanTheorem pattern. All 5 level generators moved to backend, frontend refactored to consume data via useLessonState/Redux.
changes: Created backend generator: services/lessonProcessors/questions/systemOfEquationsGenerator.js — 5 level generators + helpers, returns standardized shape with visualData, acceptedAnswers, hint, explanation
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js — added to getQuestionGenerator() chain and hasCustomGenerator() check
Updated backend config: skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for all 5 levels with batchSize 10

Refactored frontend: removed ~280 lines of client-side generation, switched to useLessonState() hook, reads from questionAnswerArray/lessonProps, uses visualData for rendering, answerType='array' with acceptedAnswers for all levels

conversations.title: Rounding — New 4-Level Lesson with Backend Data Generation
conversations.summary: Created new 4-level Rounding lesson from scratch with backend data generation. Level 1: whole number rounding (ones/tens/hundreds/thousands), Level 2: decimal rounding (ones/tenths/hundredths/thousandths), Level 3: mixed + extended place values, Level 4: word problems (money/distance/population/science/temperature).
changes: Created backend generator: roundingGenerator.js — 4 level generators with roundToPlace helper, formatNumber, getBounds, buildAcceptedAnswers, 5 word problem templates
Created backend config: rounding.config.js — skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for all 4 levels with batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js and config in lessonConfigs/index.js
Created frontend component: Rounding.jsx — single component for all 4 levels, useLessonState hook, CSS number line visualization, theme-aware styled components, answerType='array'
Updated DataLesson.js: lazy-loaded Rounding component for 4 levels
Updated algebra/index.js exports and getLessonDataInitial.js component names

conversations.title: Order of Operations — New 4-Level Interactive Lesson
conversations.summary: Created new 4-level Order of Operations lesson with interactive PEMDAS mechanic. L1-3: students click operators in correct PEMDAS order, expression simplifies step by step. L4: type the final answer. Backend generates token-based expressions with pre-computed evaluation steps.
changes: Created backend generator: orderOfOperationsGenerator.js — PEMDAS engine (computeSteps, findInnermostParenGroup, findHighestPriorityOp, collapseTokens, removeRedundantParens), 4 level generators with guards (clean division, capped exponents, non-negative intermediates, mixed priorities)
Created backend config: order_of_operations.config.js — skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for all 4 levels with batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js and config in lessonConfigs/index.js

Created frontend component: OrderOfOperations.jsx — interactive click-to-evaluate for L1-3 (token display, operator buttons, shake/collapse animations, step log), KaTeX + AnswerInput for L4, theme-aware styled components
Updated DataLesson.js: lazy-loaded OrderOfOperations component for 4 levels (replaced Evaluating)
Updated algebra/index.js exports and getLessonDataInitial.js component names

conversations.title: Venn Diagrams — 5-Level Interactive Lesson with Answer Checking
conversations.summary: Rewrote Venn Diagrams from a single free-form exploration into a 5-level graded lesson. L1-2: 2-circle drag with answer checking (4 and 6 items). L3: pre-filled Venn with counting questions. L4: 3-circle drag (8 zones). L5: set notation with probability fractions.
changes: Created backend generator: vennDiagramGenerator.js — 16 L1 templates, 15 L2 templates, 8 L3/L5 themes with name pools, 10 L4 3-circle templates, counting + set notation question builders, fraction simplification
Updated backend config: venn_diagrams.config.js — skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for 5 levels with batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js

Rewrote frontend component: VennDiagram.js — 3 modes (drag/questions/notation), Konva canvas with zone detection geometry (2-circle + 3-circle), answer checking with green/red feedback, AnswerInput for typed levels, theme-aware
Updated DataLesson.js and getLessonDataInitial.js for 5 levels

conversations.title: Basic Probability — 5-Level Interactive Lesson
conversations.summary: Created 5-level basic probability lesson. L1: marbles in bag, L2: spinner, L3: complementary events (P(NOT x)), L4: two independent events (coin+die), L5: word problems. All levels accept both simplified and unsimplified fraction answers.

conversations.title: Remove Reflection Symmetry Lesson
conversations.summary: Removed reflection_symmetry lesson (redundant with symmetry). Kept reflection lesson intact and reflection_symmetry.png image (used by symmetry and mess_around).

conversations.title: iPad Optimization — Touch-Friendly Inputs & Drag Handles
conversations.summary: iPad touch optimization complete across 5 phases. Phase 1: Created useIsTouchDevice hook, MathKeypad bottom-sheet with stacked fraction display, integrated into AnswerInput (34 consumers get it free). Phase 2: Created TouchDragHandle (22px invisible hit area + affordance ring), updated all 6 MovablePointsAndLines variants + 6 individual lessons (Angles, TriangleInequality, Symmetry, Symmetry2, Translation, Reflection). Phase 3: VennDiagram chips 1.3x larger on touch, zone tolerance 30px, touch-action CSS. Phase 4: Snap-to-grid on Translation.js for touch devices. Phase 5: Safari viewport meta (maximum-scale=1), global touch-action:manipulation on buttons, scroll-into-view when keypad opens.
phases_completed: Phase 1: useIsTouchDevice hook + MathKeypad + FractionDisplay + AnswerInput integration
Phase 2: TouchDragHandle component + 6 MovablePointsAndLines + 6 lesson updates
Phase 3: VennDiagram touch optimization + touchAction CSS on all Stage wrappers
Phase 4: Snap-to-grid on Translation + tolerance adjustments
Phase 5: Safari viewport meta, global touch CSS, keypad viewport management

conversations.title: Triangle Inequality — 5-Level Interactive Lesson Rebuild
conversations.summary: Rebuilt triangle inequality lesson from scratch with 5 interactive levels inspired by SidesAndAnglesLesson patterns. L1: drag exploration (keep existing interactive visualization). L2: Yes/No choice with shake animation. L3: tap the failing inequality check card. L4: find valid range with AnswerInput. L5: word problems with multiple choice.
changes: Created backend generator: triangleInequalityGenerator.js — 5 level generators (explore, yes/no, which-fails, range, word problems) with valid/invalid triangle generators, shuffle, word problem templates (fence/path/beam/straw/rope)
Updated backend config: triangle_inequality.config.js — skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for all 5 levels with batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js — added to getQuestionGenerator() chain and hasCustomGenerator() check

Rewrote frontend component: TriangleInequality.js — single component for all 5 levels, useLessonState hook, L1 self-contained drag (ExploreLevel subcomponent with TouchDragHandle), L2/L5 choice buttons with shake/fade animations, L3 check cards with fail/pass indicators, L4 AnswerInput for range entry, phase-based flow (interact→complete), theme-aware styled components, dark mode compatible
Updated DataLesson.js: 5 levels all using TriangleInequality component (was 2 levels with BasicProblemsWordsOnly + TriangleInequality)
Updated getLessonDataInitial.js: 5 component entries for TriangleInequality

conversations.title: Triangle Sum — Complete 5-Level Lesson Rebuild
conversations.summary: Rebuilt Triangle Sum lesson from scratch as a 5-level interactive lesson. L1: drag vertex explore (angles sum to 180°, make a right triangle). L2: find missing angle (type answer). L3: solve for x (algebraic expression). L4: classify triangle (acute/right/obtuse choice). L5: word problems (multiple choice). Same architecture as Triangle Inequality rebuild.
changes: Created new standalone backend generator: triangleSumGenerator.js with 5-level generation (explore, missing angle, solve-for-x, classification, word problems)
Updated triangle_sum.config.js: 5 levels, skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], custom data generation for all levels
Registered new generator in lessonProcessors/index.js (before angleQuestionGenerator to intercept triangle_sum)
Rewrote TriangleSum.js frontend: 5-level interactive component with Konva canvas, TouchDragHandle, phase-based flow, shake animations, dark mode support
Level 1: draggable vertex with real-time angle computation, sum display, right triangle detection and completion
Levels 2-3: triangle canvas with angles computed from backend data using triangleFromAngles positioning
Levels 4-5: choice buttons with shake/fade animations
Updated DataLesson.js: 5 levels all using TriangleSum component
Updated getLessonDataInitial.js: 5 component entries for TriangleSum

conversations.title: Area — 9-Level Interactive Lesson
conversations.summary: Created 9-level Area lesson. L1: drag to make target area. L2: count squares (MC). L3: rectangles (l×w). L4: triangles as rectangle cut in half. L5: rectangles & triangles no formula help. L6: trapezoids split into two triangles. L7: parallelograms (b×h). L8: all shapes no help. L9: word problems (MC). Konva canvas with shape-specific visualizations, dark mode compatible.
changes: Rewrote backend generator: areaGenerator.js with 9 levels (target area, count squares, rectangles, triangle-from-rect, mixed rect/tri, trapezoids, parallelograms, all shapes, word problems)
Updated area.config.js: 9 levels with custom data generation

Rewrote frontend component: Area.js — 9-level interactive component with Konva canvas: grid drag for L1, rectangle grid for L2, rectangle/triangle/trapezoid/parallelogram shape rendering for L3-8, choice buttons for L2/L9, AnswerInput for L3-L8
Updated DataLesson.js and getLessonDataInitial.js for 9 levels

conversations.title: Composite Shapes — 8-Level Interactive Lesson (From Scratch)
conversations.summary: Built composite shapes lesson from scratch as an 8-level interactive lesson, replacing the old 3-component setup (CompositeShape/2/3). L1: identify shapes (MC). L2: L-shapes with split line (addition). L3: T-shapes & U-shapes. L4: cutout shapes (subtraction). L5: rectangles + triangles (house/arrow). L6: mixed composite shapes (no help). L7: find missing dimension (given area). L8: word problems (MC). Backend data generation with Konva canvas polygon rendering.
changes: Created backend generator: compositeShapeGenerator.js — 8 level generators with shape builders (L, T, U, cutout, house, arrow), wrong answer generation, MC choice builders, word problem templates (garden, hallway, house wall, patio, L-office, path around field)
Updated composite_shapes.config.js: 8 levels with custom data generation, skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js — added to getQuestionGenerator() chain and hasCustomGenerator() check

Created new frontend component: CompositeShapeLesson.js — single component for all 8 levels with Konva polygon rendering (buildLPolygon, buildTPolygon, buildUPolygon, cutout/house/arrow renderers), MC choice buttons for L1/L8, AnswerInput for L2-L7, formula hints for L2/L4/L5/L7, phase-based flow, theme-aware styled components
Added CompositeShapeLesson to geometry/index.js exports
Updated DataLesson.js: lazy-loaded CompositeShapeLessonComp for 8 levels (replaced old CompositeShape/2/3)
Updated getLessonDataInitial.js: 8 CompositeShapeLesson entries with updated hints

conversations.title: SAS & SSS — Dynamic Triangle Positioning Implementation (COMPLETED)
conversations.summary: Implemented dynamic triangle positioning for both SAS and SSS congruent triangle lessons. Triangles now position based on actual bounding boxes (including labels/annotations) instead of hardcoded coordinates. PRIMARY GOAL: Triangles are positioned significantly higher on screen (~60-70px higher). SECONDARY: Minimum 50px horizontal gap between triangles, algorithm-optimized spacing.
changes: Added positioning constants to both generators: CANVAS_DESIGN_WIDTH=400, CANVAS_DESIGN_HEIGHT=360, MIN_TOP_MARGIN=40, MIN_HORIZONTAL_GAP=50, TRIANGLE_PADDING=30, ANNOTATION_BUFFER=35
Created shared helper functions: getTriangleBounds(), getTriangleBoundsWithAnnotations(), positionTrianglesSideBySide(), positionTrianglesGrid()
Updated generateTriangleVertices() to generate at origin (0,0) instead of taking cx,cy parameters
SAS Level 1, 2, 4: Updated to use positionTrianglesSideBySide() for dynamic side-by-side positioning
SAS Level 3: Updated to use positionTrianglesGrid() for 2x2 grid with all 4 pairs visible
SSS Level 1, 2, 4: Updated to use positionTrianglesSideBySide() for dynamic side-by-side positioning
SSS Level 3: Updated to use positionTrianglesGrid() for 2x2 grid with all 4 pairs visible
All triangles now positioned ~60-70px higher (from y=120 or y=180 to y=~40-50)
Horizontal spacing increased from ~170px gap to minimum 50px with algorithm optimization
Frontend requires NO changes - existing responsive scaling works with new backend positions

how_to_reuse: Copy the CONSTANTS section from sasCongruentTrianglesGenerator.js
Copy the BOUNDING BOX AND POSITIONING section (all 4 helper functions)
Update generateTriangleVertices() signature to remove cx, cy parameters
Update level generators: generate triangles at origin, then call positioning helpers

key_improvements: ✓ PRIMARY GOAL ACHIEVED: Triangles positioned 60-70px higher (y=120→y=40-50)
✓ SECONDARY GOAL ACHIEVED: Minimum 50px horizontal gap with smart spacing
✓ All 4 triangle pairs in grid view visible without scrolling
✓ Annotations (labels, angle arcs) accounted for in bounding box calculations
✓ Dynamic positioning ensures triangles never go outside canvas bounds
✓ Works across all screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop) via frontend scaling
✓ Consistent positioning approach across SAS and SSS lessons
testing_notes: Backend servers need restart to load updated generators
Verify triangles are higher and well-spaced across all 5 levels of both lessons
Check that labels and angle indicators don't get cut off
Confirm all 4 pairs visible in Level 3 grid without scrolling

conversations.title: Proportional Reasoning — New 5-Level Conceptual Lesson
conversations.summary: Created new 5-level Proportional Reasoning lesson (separate from Proportions). Focus on conceptual understanding of proportional relationships through visual models, ratio tables, unit rates, and identifying proportional vs non-proportional relationships. Progressive difficulty from visual/concrete to abstract reasoning.
changes: Created backend generator: proportionalReasoningGenerator.js — 5 level generators (visual ratios MC, ratio tables input, unit rates input, proportional check MC, word problems input), includes visual data for colored block displays
Created backend config: proportional_reasoning.config.js — skipSteps [3,5,6,7,8,9,10,11], customDataGeneration enabled for 5 levels with batchSize 10
Registered generator in lessonProcessors/index.js and config in lessonConfigs/index.js

Created frontend component: ProportionalReasoning.jsx — single component for all 5 levels with visual ratio display (colored blocks), ratio tables, proportional checking, dark mode compatible, theme-aware styled components
L1: Visual Ratios — identify equivalent ratios from colored block models (MC)
L2: Ratio Tables — complete missing values in proportional tables (input)
L3: Unit Rates — find the constant of proportionality (input)
L4: Proportional vs Non-Proportional — identify relationships by checking if ratio stays constant (MC)
L5: Real-World Applications — recipes, speeds, prices, workers scaling word problems (input)
Updated DataLesson.js: lazy-loaded ProportionalReasoning for 5 levels
Updated algebra/index.js exports and getLessonDataInitial.js hints

conversations.title: Reflection Lesson — iPad Single-Screen Optimization
conversations.summary: Multi-bot analysis of all 5 reflection levels to ensure everything fits on one iPad screen without scrolling. Identified vertical space issues (800-1150px content in 768-1024px viewport) and created comprehensive optimization plan.

level1_vertical_reflection.bot_recommendations: Compact header, hint popover, modal explanation, reduced canvas padding

level2_horizontal_reflection.issues: Fits on iPad but tight margins
level2_horizontal_reflection.bot_recommendations: Preventive spacing reduction, already mostly optimized

level3_combined_reflection.issues: Borderline - exceeds with all UI shown
level3_combined_reflection.bot_recommendations: Reduce canvas to 400px, slide-in explanation panel, compact instruction text

level4_plotting_reflections.bot_recommendations: Dynamic canvas sizing when keypad opens (500px → 300px), keypad-aware layout

level5_diagonal_reflection.issues: Exceeds viewport by up to 54px with all UI
level5_diagonal_reflection.bot_recommendations: Systematic spacing reduction, inline Try Another button, 450px canvas

optimization_phases: Phase 1: Universal spacing reduction (120-150px savings across all levels)
Phase 2: Dynamic canvas sizing (50-100px savings, responsive to device)
Phase 3: Modal explanation (150-205px savings, overlay instead of inline)
Phase 4: Hint popover (60-80px savings when hint shown)
Phase 5: Level 4 keypad-aware canvas (200px savings when typing)

description: Active conversations less than 7 days old
